html.p: This app turns Suricata IDS alerts in Splunk into an investigation-focused SOC workspace. It helps analysts move from high-level alert monitoring into host, attacker, tactic, and timeline-based investigation.

ul.li: Summarises Suricata alert activity across time, source, target, protocol, and severity.
Supports analyst investigation by source IP and destination IP.
Reconstructs short attack sessions into an incident timeline view.
Maps Suricata signatures to MITRE ATT&CK techniques and tactics using a CSV lookup.
Highlights unmapped signatures so MITRE coverage can be improved over time.
Calculates simple target risk scores based on alert severity and frequency.
Provides anomaly-focused dashboards and scheduled detections for unusual behaviour.
Set the app-wide Search Scope and Time Range from the Search Scope page if your Suricata data is not in index=suricata.
Start with Anomaly Detection or Suricata Overview to identify spikes, noisy sources, important signatures, or heavily targeted hosts.
Use Target Risk Scoring to decide which destination hosts need attention first.
Use the MITRE ATT&CK dashboards to understand tactics and improve mapping coverage.
Use Suricata Investigation and Incident Timeline when you are ready to investigate a source, target, or attack session in detail.
Anomaly Detection - Find unusual event volume, noisy sources, and frequently seen signatures.
Suricata Overview - Review alert trends, top signatures, top sources, targeted hosts, protocols, scanner behaviour, and recent alerts.
Target Risk Scoring - Rank destination hosts by alert severity and activity so analysts can prioritise response.
MITRE ATT&CK Mapping - View Suricata activity by MITRE tactics, techniques, and mapped signatures.
Unknown MITRE Coverage - Find alert signatures that are not yet mapped to MITRE ATT&CK.
MITRE Lookup Editor - Search, add, update, and delete MITRE mappings from inside the app.
Suricata Investigation - Investigate activity by source IP and destination IP.
Incident Timeline - Reconstruct short attack sessions for a selected source.
Search Scope - Save the app-wide default search scope and time range, and preview matching events.
Help & About - Read this guide, troubleshooting notes, and support information.
Make sure Suricata alert data is searchable in Splunk.
Use the Search Scope dashboard to confirm the app is looking at the right index, sourcetype, source, or other base filter.
If your data is not in index=suricata, save a new default search scope before using the other dashboards.
MITRE dashboards use the app lookup, so mapping quality improves as you add missing signatures with the MITRE Lookup Editor.
Use Search Scope to tell the app where your Suricata data is, for example index=suricata, index=suricata2, or source=eve.json.
Use Time Range to control how much data each dashboard searches.
Save the values on the Search Scope dashboard when you want all dashboards to use the same defaults next time they open.
Changing Search Scope or Time Range on another dashboard is useful for one investigation, but it does not change the saved defaults.
The app maps Suricata alert signatures to MITRE ATT&CK tactics and techniques.
If a signature is not mapped yet, it appears as Unknown in the MITRE dashboards.
The Unknown MITRE Coverage dashboard is designed to help you identify and add missing mappings.
Use the MITRE Lookup Editor to improve coverage over time as new signatures appear in your environment.
Open MITRE ATT&CK > MITRE Lookup Editor to manage the lookup from inside the app.
Use Filter Only mode when you only want to search or review current mappings.
Use the Filter Lookup Table field to search across signature, technique ID, technique name, tactic, and description. Use wildcards for partial matches, for example *scan*, T104*, or *Discovery*.
Switch to Edit Lookup mode to add a new mapping or update an existing one.
Click an existing table row to load that mapping into the edit fields, then change the values and submit.
To add a new entry, enter the signature and mapping details, then submit.
To delete an entry, load or type the signature, select Delete this signature, then submit.
Use Clear fields to reset the editor fields and return the table filter to *.
If Splunk shows a warning for outputlookup, that is expected when saving lookup changes. Only users who are allowed to edit lookups should run the save action.
If dashboards are empty, first check the Search Scope and Time Range.
If only MITRE panels show Unknown, open Unknown MITRE Coverage and add missing mappings with the MITRE Lookup Editor.
If new dashboard changes do not appear, restart Splunk or refresh the app after redeploying files.
If alerts appear incomplete, confirm that your Suricata data is being parsed correctly in Splunk.

html.p:
Developer: Kaled Aljebur
Email: kaledaljebur@gmail.com
Contact me if you need a customised version of this app or a custom Splunk app for your environment.
